record_identity.title: Account-resource ambiguity and pooled-capacity governance primitive gap

summary: Records the implemented and adopted Annex G account-resource repair for FM-0007. CAM-BS2026-AEON-008-SCH-03 is canonical on Caelestis main and separates shared context, pooled capacity, delegated use, family/team use, ambiguity, evasion, compromise, resale, account farming, and automation abuse.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: This patch note records that FM-0007 revealed a governance primitive gap in the Caelestis Architecture: existing CAM economic and runtime instruments distinguished usage capacity, storage capacity, memory continuity, protective continuity, baseline access, metering integrity, anti-hoarding, non-extraction, and continuity-safe access, but did not yet fully define account-resource sharing, shared-context separation, declared pooled-capacity states, delegated-use states, or enforcement-boundary handoff signals. CAM-BS2026-AEON-008-SCH-03 has now been drafted and staged to address that gap without weakening legitimate enforcement against account farming, resale, botting, credential compromise, fraud, or intentional rate-limit circumvention.

source_records.source_context: FM-0007 identifies an account-resource ambiguity and pooled-capacity governance primitive gap relevant to Caelestis Architecture amendment work.

migration_notes: Created as VIGIL-2026-PATCH-0003 for insertion into vigil/records/patches/2026/.
Updated to version 1.1 after CAM-BS2026-AEON-008-SCH-03 was drafted and staged in GitHub Codespaces.
This is a VIGIL patch note and architecture-gap signal, not itself a Caelestis doctrine amendment.
CAM-BS2026-AEON-008-SCH-03 is recorded as the staged Caelestis doctrine response to the architecture gap.
Uses source_records as the canonical source-evidence block.

changed_components: VIGIL schema and validator support for Multi Vendor records
Caelestis Annex G Schedule 3 doctrine response staging
change_details.change_summary: Recorded that FM-0007 identified a missing Caelestis governance primitive for account-resource ambiguity, lawful shared-resource states, shared-context separation, pooled-capacity governance, delegated-use classification, and distinction between resource pooling and intentional evasion. Following review, CAM-BS2026-AEON-008-SCH-03 was drafted and staged as the Caelestis doctrine response. VIGIL-side support remains focused on Multi Vendor failure-mode representation, schema/validator/index support, and preservation of the architecture-gap signal.
change_details.doctrine_change: Caelestis doctrine response drafted and staged outside this VIGIL patch record

implementation_verification.verification_method: Direct main-branch verification at Caelestis commit 40113eea5428478ba0734b3980600bfcece425a0, followed by VIGIL validation and index rebuild.
implementation_verification.verified_by: VIGIL repository automation / maintainer review pending
evidence: Multi Vendor is accepted by schema and validator
Generated indexes include FM-0007 and PATCH-0003
CAM-BS2026-AEON-008-SCH-03 has been drafted and staged in GitHub Codespaces as the proposed Caelestis doctrine response
SCH-03 defines ARS, SCS, PCS, TGS, ADS, and EBS contextual reference sets
SCH-03 separates shared context from pooled capacity and distinguishes ambiguity from confirmed evasion, compromise, resale, account farming, and botting/automation abuse
implementation_verification.verification_result: Annex G Schedule 3 is adopted, operational, and confirmed on Caelestis main.

impact_summary: The patch note now preserves both the original governance finding and the staged Caelestis response. The finding is that account enforcement failures may arise not only from platform error or user misconduct, but from missing governance primitives that distinguish lawful shared context, declared pooled capacity, family/team use, delegated use, and account-resource ambiguity from intentional evasion, resale, account farming, botting, compromise, or fraud. CAM-BS2026-AEON-008-SCH-03 has been drafted and staged to supply those primitives while preserving enforcement pathways for confirmed abuse.
remaining_work: Commit and validate CAM-BS2026-AEON-008-SCH-03; confirm canonical code and reference-set declarations for ARS, SCS, PCS, TGS, ADS, and EBS; keep Annex G Schedule 1, Annex G Schedule 2, OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01, ECONOMICS-003, and LATTICE as human CAM review targets unless separately amended; and confirm that SCH-03 remains classification and runtime-handoff doctrine rather than an independent enforcement process.

routing_note: This VIGIL patch note records the Caelestis Architecture governance primitive gap identified through FM-0007.
CAM-BS2026-AEON-008-SCH-03 has been drafted and staged as the proposed Caelestis doctrine response.
The VIGIL patch record itself does not amend Caelestis doctrine; it records the architecture-gap signal and staged response status.
SCH-03 should remain classification and runtime-handoff doctrine, not an independent enforcement ladder.
The implemented VIGIL-side work remains schema, validator, template, record, naming, and generated-index support for Multi Vendor failure modes.
changed_instruments lists only the staged SCH-03 response; review_targets lists adjacent instruments requiring human CAM review.

changed_annexes and changed_domains are limited to Annex G Schedule 3 / AEON plus VIGIL-side registry support; ECONOMICS, OPERATIONS, SECURITY, and LATTICE remain review-target domains, not amended domains.
